mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without overheating, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns are negative when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following positive industrial production revisions over a 2-year evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates a sudden contraction in consumer spending, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >65% of instances following a retail sales drop exceeding the threshold over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences over a 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods following inflation spikes above the threshold over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in monthly housing starts signal expanding residential construction activity and rising consumer confidence, which generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods following positive housing start revisions over a 2-year evaluation.
